was the rrTon clark for City CouncilI committee, which renains
extant- This year you campaigned unsuccessfully for the office of
nayor; your controlled cornmittee in the mayoral contest was the
rrclark for ltayorrr connittee, whj-ch has a current deficit. 
You
would like to retire the defj,cit from the nayoral funds with funds
from the "Ton clark for City Councilrr comrnittee.
ANALYSIS
The Act, as amended by Proposition 73, prohibits transfers of
funds between controlled cornrnittees. However, on May 15, 1989,
the United states District court, Eastern District of California
enjoined the comnission fron enforcing that portion of Section
85304 which prohibited a candidate or elected officer fron
transferring funds among his or her own controlled committees.
(service Enplovees Internationa] Union. AFLo-cIo, et aI. v. Fair
PoIitical Practices Comrnission (case No. CfVS-89-0433 LKX-JFM
('tsEIUx).) Therefore, you may transfer funds frorn your city
council comnittee to the "clark for Itlayor committee."
Regarding the above, there is one important caveat. Section
a5ao7, which deals with distribution of surplus campaign funds
remaining at the conclusion of the post-election reporting period
following the defeat of a candidate, does not al-l-ow a transfer of
those surplus funds to a committee which supports or opposes a
candidate for eLective office. 
what that rneans is that you may
transfer councj-] comnittee funds to the mayoral committee account,
but you may not transfer mayoral comrnittee funds to the council
comrnittee account once those funds have become "surplus funds.rl
(See Leece Advi.ce Letter, No. I-90-061, copy enclosed. )
The injunction handed down in the SEIU case is a prelirninary
injunction; the Commission is awaiting a final ruling from the
court, which may or may not deem the challenged sections of
Proposition 73, including the transfer provision,
unconstitutional . Until the court nakes its ruling, ho$/ever, our
advice is that a transfer such as the one you contemplate, frorn
your council committee to your mayoral comrnittee is pennitted.
(See Maron Advice Letter, No. I-90-2o2, copy enclosed. )
Although a transfer of funds to pay debts of your nayoral
race is pernitted under the existing injunction, certain other
restrictions of Proposition 73 relnain in effect. 
For example, the
contribution Iimitation of $1,000 (Section 85301) from any one
person to a candidate in any fiscal year has not changed not-
withstandingT any transfer(s) of funds to another controlled con-
nittee. 
Thus, if contributor "A" gives $5oo to your council com-
mittee, and you inrnediately transfer the contribution, you v/ould
not be able to accept nrore than an addi.tional $500 fron
contributor rAtr in the same fiscal year that the first contribu-
tion was made.


Our Fi■e No. A-90-432
Page 3
SH:SLB:p■h
Enc■osures
Sincerely,
Scott Hallabrin

f hoPe this response answers your question. If you have

funds, please do not hesitate to call ne at (916) 322-59oL.
